Application version is defined.functional: Application reports a valid version string so users and logs can identify the running version

Idle state shows no capture message.functional: Status bar shows 'No capture' when app has not captured anything

Capturing state shows progress message.functional: Status bar indicates capture is in progress

Captured state shows dimensions.functional: After capture, status bar shows image dimensions

RegionSelector can be instantiated.technical: RegionSelector can be created without parameters

RegionSelector cancel calls completion w.functional: ESC during region selection cancels and returns to idle
RegionSelector cancel calls completion w.technical: RegionSelector.cancel() calls completion handler with nil CGRect

flipAndClampRect flips Y-axis from AppKi.functional: Region selection coordinates are correctly translated for capture
flipAndClampRect flips Y-axis from AppKi.technical: flipAndClampRect converts AppKit origin (bottom-left) to CG origin (top-left)

flipAndClampRect handles origin at botto.functional: Region at the bottom of the screen maps to top in CG coords
flipAndClampRect handles origin at botto.technical: AppKit y=0 (bottom) flips to y=displayHeight-height (top) in CG

flipAndClampRect clamps rect that extend.functional: A selection that partially extends beyond the screen is clipped
flipAndClampRect clamps rect that extend.technical: Rect extending past display edge is intersected with display bounds

flipAndClampRect returns nil for fully o.functional: A selection completely outside the screen is rejected
flipAndClampRect returns nil for fully o.technical: flipAndClampRect returns nil when rect has no overlap with display

flipAndClampRect handles zero-size displ.functional: No crash on edge case with zero-size display

flipAndClampRect preserves full-screen r.functional: Selecting the entire screen produces valid coordinates
flipAndClampRect preserves full-screen r.technical: Full-screen AppKit rect flips to (0,0) in CG with same dimensions

flipAndClampRect handles rect on seconda.functional: Region capture works on secondary screen with display offset
flipAndClampRect handles rect on seconda.technical: Rect relative to a secondary display flips Y correctly within that display's bounds

flipAndClampRect clamps rect extending b.functional: Region at top edge of screen is clipped correctly
flipAndClampRect clamps rect extending b.technical: AppKit rect extending beyond top of display is clamped after Y-flip

availableWindows returns non-empty list.functional: User sees a list of available windows to capture

availableWindows returns non-empty list.expectedOutput: Non-empty [WindowInfo] with valid titles and positive frame dimensions

captureRegion captures a valid image.expectedOutput: CGImage with positive width/height, CaptureResult.target == .region with matching rect

Window capture contains correct QR code.functional: A captured window contains all its visual content, verifiable by QR decode
Window capture contains correct QR code.technical: Create reference window with CIQRCodeGenerator QR encoding a UUID, capture via ScreenCaptureKitProvider.captureWindow, decode with VNDetectBarcodesRequest, compare payload to original UUID
Window capture contains correct QR code.input: NSWindow (400x400, .floating) with QR code encoding UUID, captured by window ID match
Window capture contains correct QR code.expectedOutput: VNDetectBarcodesRequest decodes QR payload identical to the generated UUID

Region capture contains correct QR code.functional: A captured screen region contains the exact content at that position
Region capture contains correct QR code.technical: Create reference window at known screen position, capture that region via captureRegion(window.frame), decode QR with VNDetectBarcodesRequest, compare to original UUID
Region capture contains correct QR code.input: NSWindow (400x400, .floating, center of primary screen), region capture of window.frame
Region capture contains correct QR code.expectedOutput: VNDetectBarcodesRequest decodes QR payload identical to the generated UUID

Region capture has colored corners in co.functional: Captured image is not cropped or shifted — all four corners are present and correctly positioned
Region capture has colored corners in co.technical: Create reference window with 40pt colored corners (red TL, green TR, blue BL, yellow BR), capture content rect via captureRegion, sample pixels at 10pt inset from each corner using scale-aware coordinates, compare RGB values with tolerance 80 for macOS P3 color space
Region capture has colored corners in co.input: NSWindow (400x400) with colored corners, region capture of contentLayoutRect in screen coords
Region capture has colored corners in co.expectedOutput: Top-left pixel near (255,0,0), top-right near (0,255,0), bottom-left near (0,0,255), bottom-right near (255,255,0) within tolerance 80

Region capture contains recognizable tex.functional: Captured region text is readable by OCR — full content integrity
Region capture contains recognizable tex.technical: Create reference window with known label 'CortexVision Verify' (16pt bold), capture region via captureRegion(window.frame), run VNRecognizeTextRequest with .accurate level, verify recognized text contains expected words
Region capture contains recognizable tex.input: NSWindow (400x400, .floating) with text label 'CortexVision Verify', region capture of window.frame
Region capture contains recognizable tex.expectedOutput: Joined OCR candidates contain both 'CortexVision' and 'Verify'

Text to the right of figure → adjacentRi.functional: Text positioned to the right of a figure is classified as adjacentRight, ensuring only the right edge expansion is blocked

Text below figure → adjacentBelow.functional: Text positioned below a figure is classified as adjacentBelow, blocking only bottom expansion
Text below figure → adjacentBelow.technical: Figure at y=0.40..0.90, text at y=0.35. classifyTextRelation returns .adjacentBelow

Text above figure → adjacentAbove.functional: Text positioned above a figure is classified as adjacentAbove

Text overlapping figure → overlapping.functional: Text inside a figure region is classified as overlapping
Text overlapping figure → overlapping.technical: Text rect fully inside figure rect. classifyTextRelation returns .overlapping
